parentQuestion: The obturator and femoral nerves branch from which plexus?

scaffolds.q: The femoral nerve supplies muscles that straighten the knee.
scaffolds.explain: The femoral nerve innervates the quadriceps muscles on the front of the thigh. The quadriceps extend (straighten) the knee — absolutely essential for walking, climbing stairs, standing up from a chair. Damage the femoral nerve, and your knee buckles when you try to walk. Take a moment to appreciate this:drawn into this dependency — one nerve, complete control of knee extension...

scaffolds.q: The lumbar plexus is formed from spinal nerves in the lower back.
scaffolds.explain: The lumbar plexus is formed from spinal nerves L1-L4 in the lumbar (lower back) region. It supplies the lower abdomen and anterior/medial thigh — the machinery for kicking and squeezing your legs together. I want you to see how this network divides its territory...
